title: How to Ace Any Post-UTME in 30 Days
summary: A proven 30-day preparation strategy for Post-UTME screening — covering time management, past questions, subject focus and mental readiness.
body: Week 1: Gather all materials. Download past questions for your school from the BQM eLibrary. Identify the subjects tested and the question format. Week 2: Practice 30 questions per day. Focus on Use of English — it appears in every Post-UTME. Week 3: Simulate exam conditions. Set a timer, sit in a quiet place and attempt full past papers. Week 4: Review your weak areas. Take extra time on topics you consistently get wrong. On exam day: sleep early, eat light, arrive 30 minutes before time.

title: UNILAG Post-UTME — What to Expect and How to Prepare
summary: A school-specific guide for candidates sitting the University of Lagos Post-UTME screening — format, subjects, time allocation and key tips.
body: UNILAG Post-UTME is an online CBT with 50 questions to be answered in 30 minutes. The exam covers Use of English (20 questions) and 2 subject areas relevant to your course (30 questions total). For Medicine: English + Biology + Chemistry. For Engineering: English + Mathematics + Physics. For Law: English + Literature + Government. Tips: Practice speed — 30 minutes for 50 questions means 36 seconds per question. Don't spend too long on one question. Flag and return. Your final score = 50% JAMB + 50% O'Level.

title: Mastering Use of English for Post-UTME
summary: English Language appears in every Post-UTME. This guide covers the most tested areas — comprehension, grammar, vocabulary and essay writing.
body: Use of English is always the highest-weighted subject in Post-UTME. Focus on: (1) Reading Comprehension — read the passage twice before answering. (2) Lexis & Structure — learn common idioms, phrasal verbs and grammatical rules. (3) Oral English — phonetics and stress patterns appear frequently. (4) Summary Writing — be concise and use your own words. Practice at least 20 English questions every day for 3 weeks. JAMB past question Use of English sections are excellent practice material.
